channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Xi Jinping To Present China's AI Dream At Major Conference Today

media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping will speak at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai on Friday.

item.description: President Xi Jinping presents China's vision for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence on Friday, at a major conference showcasing cutting-edge technology he hopes will soon rival that of the United States.
item.content:encoded: President Xi Jinping presents China's vision for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence on Friday, at a major conference showcasing cutting-edge technology he hopes will soon rival that of the United States.

item.title: "Crude And Arrogant": Brazil Hits Out At Marco Rubio Over Remarks On Lula

media:content.media:description: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira said Rubio's remarks were "unacceptable and offensive."

item.description: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira on Thursday accused his US counterpart Marco Rubio of "attacking" President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva in a "crude and arrogant" manner when commenting on a new tariff decision.
item.content:encoded: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira on Thursday accused his US counterpart Marco Rubio of "attacking" President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva in a "crude and arrogant" manner when commenting on a new tariff decision.

item.title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Launches 5th Consecutive Night Of Strikes Against Iran
item.description: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

item.title: 'SAARC, BIMSTEC Don't Need To Be Rivals': Bangladesh At Delhi Meet

media:content.media:description: This isn't the first time Dhaka has brought SAARC up.

item.description: According to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, the meeting pulled together security advisers and heads of delegations from all six other member states, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka and Thailand.
item.content:encoded: According to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, the meeting pulled together security advisers and heads of delegations from all six other member states, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka and Thailand.

item.title: Trump Administration Tightens Visa Limits For Foreign Students And Journalists

media:content.media:description: Foreign journalists would be limited to stays of just 240 days, or around eight months

item.description: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years.
item.content:encoded: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years.

item.title: Trump To Accuse China Of Meddling In US Elections In Primetime Speech: Report

media:content.media:description: Trump has claimed that the 2020 presidential election was stolen because of fraud

item.description: Several of the country's top national security officials are expected to attend the speech. Those invited include the heads of the CIA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence, Department of Homeland Security and other senior officials.
item.content:encoded: Several of the country's top national security officials are expected to attend the speech. Those invited include the heads of the CIA, FBI, Office of the Director of National Intelligence, Department of Homeland Security and other senior officials.

item.title: EU Report Raises Rights Concerns As Pakistan Reaps Billions In Trade Benefits

media:content.media:description: Looking ahead, EU officials say Islamabad must show real accountability for rights violations

item.description: Pakistan pocketed roughly "732 million in EU tariff exemptions last year, but a new joint assessment from the European Commission and the EU's foreign policy arm paints a troubling picture of the human rights record.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan pocketed roughly "732 million in EU tariff exemptions last year, but a new joint assessment from the European Commission and the EU's foreign policy arm paints a troubling picture of the human rights record.

item.title: Troops In Hormuz Islands, Bombing Pickaxe Mountain: Trump's Big Iran War Plan

media:content.media:description: One of the biggest proposals under discussion is an operation to seize Kharg Island

item.description: Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options.
item.content:encoded: Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options.

item.title: 11 Killed In Algerian Orphanage Fire Amid Severe Heatwave

media:content.media:description: Northern Algeria struck by forest fires, caused by drought and climate change.

item.description: Firefighters had to use a chainsaw to remove metal bars from a window, say witnesses.
item.content:encoded: Firefighters had to use a chainsaw to remove metal bars from a window, say witnesses.

item.title: "Trying To Change Public Opinion": JD Vance On Israel's Influence In Iran Deal

media:content.media:description: JD Vance defended a deal reached last month to end the war with Iran. (File)

item.description: JD Vance said that there are some people within their system that are manipulating the American public opinion to keep the war going on indefinitely.
item.content:encoded: JD Vance said that there are some people within their system that are manipulating the American public opinion to keep the war going on indefinitely.

item.title: 'Take Off Your Uniform And Fight Elections': Pakistan MP Dares Asim Munir

media:content.media:description: Maulana Fazlur Rehman accused the military of interfering in Pak's political system

item.description: Addressing a public gathering in Pakistan on Sunday, Jamiat Ulema-e-Islam-F (JUI-F) chief Maulana Fazlur Rehman said, "If you want to do politics, then take off your uniform and come. Take part in the election. You will get to know how many votes people in uniform receive."
item.content:encoded: Addressing a public gathering in Pakistan on Sunday, Jamiat Ulema-e-Islam-F (JUI-F) chief Maulana Fazlur Rehman said, "If you want to do politics, then take off your uniform and come. Take part in the election. You will get to know how many votes people in uniform receive."

item.title: Watch: Deer Seek Refuge On Iran's Kharg Island Amid US Bombing Explosions

media:content.media:description: Explosions from US bombs cause deer to seek refuge in streets on Iran's Kharg Island.

item.description: Deer on Kharg Island run into streets amid US bomb explosions, seeking refuge from attacks.
item.content:encoded: Deer on Kharg Island run into streets amid US bomb explosions, seeking refuge from attacks.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Was A Russian Doomsday Plane In Iran Recently? Inside The 'Tu-214PU'
item.description: A Russian Tu-214PU aircraft took off from Moscow and landed in Tehran at around 10:10 am on July 13. The deployment coincided with escalating US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure
item.content:encoded: A Russian Tu-214PU aircraft took off from Moscow and landed in Tehran at around 10:10 am on July 13. The deployment coincided with escalating US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure

item.title: How A German App Is Turning Shade Into A Lifeline During Heatwaves

media:content.media:description: Adapting to climate change is no longer a future concern but a public health challenge.

item.description: As Europe reels from extreme heatwaves, a German app is helping people stay cool by mapping shaded walking routes. The app uses sun and shadow data to guide commuters away from heat-exposed streets.
item.content:encoded: As Europe reels from extreme heatwaves, a German app is helping people stay cool by mapping shaded walking routes. The app uses sun and shadow data to guide commuters away from heat-exposed streets.

item.title: Top AI Models Are Less Critical Of Repressive Regimes: Meta Report

media:content.media:description: AI companies urged to conduct systematic human rights analyses. (Representational)

item.description: A study showed AI services were echoing the rules of countries that restrict speech and that bias could creep into services used by an increasing number of users.
item.content:encoded: A study showed AI services were echoing the rules of countries that restrict speech and that bias could creep into services used by an increasing number of users.

item.title: 'Cowardly Crime Against Children': Iran Slams US Attack Near Cancer Hospital

media:content.media:description: The hospital which provided chemotherapy treatment for cancer patients had to be evacuated

item.description: Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said that the attack was "reminiscent of Israel's atrocities against healthcare facilities". He said that the attack caused severe suffering and anxiety to the children who were hospitalised.
item.content:encoded: Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said that the attack was "reminiscent of Israel's atrocities against healthcare facilities". He said that the attack caused severe suffering and anxiety to the children who were hospitalised.

item.title: New Zealand Issues Tsunami Warning After 6.3-Magnitude Earthquake
item.description: The epicenter of the earthquake was about 40 km north of Te Anau, which is the gateway to the tourist hotspot of Fiordland.
item.content:encoded: The epicenter of the earthquake was about 40 km north of Te Anau, which is the gateway to the tourist hotspot of Fiordland.

item.title: US Mint Begins Production Of Controversial $1 Coin Bearing Trump's Face

media:content.media:description: The front of the coin features an image of Trump in a suit and tie and with a stern look on his face.

item.description: The coin is intended "to honor the enduring legacy of liberty and a lasting symbol of patriotism," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.content:encoded: The coin is intended "to honor the enduring legacy of liberty and a lasting symbol of patriotism," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

item.title: US Considers Permanent Daylight Saving Time Again. How It Could Work

media:content.media:description: Currently, the shift is forward in spring and back to standard time in fall. (Representational)

item.description: The House of Representatives on Tuesday voted to pass a bill to shift daylight saving time, when clocks are moved forward one hour, become permanent.
item.content:encoded: The House of Representatives on Tuesday voted to pass a bill to shift daylight saving time, when clocks are moved forward one hour, become permanent.

item.title: "We Will Kill Trump": Iran's New Billboard Puts US President In A Coffin

media:content.media:description: Iran has long used public spaces as a tool of political communication.

item.description: The new hoarding shows the US president's body peeking out from an open black coffin. In the picture, his hair is disheveled, and his eyes and mouth are closed, as his hands rest on a red tie atop a protruding stomach.
item.content:encoded: The new hoarding shows the US president's body peeking out from an open black coffin. In the picture, his hair is disheveled, and his eyes and mouth are closed, as his hands rest on a red tie atop a protruding stomach.
